data.content:
The build generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` but the compiler doesn't see it. The problem is that **MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file created during the `GenerateSampleCode` target is never added to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to explicitly add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` within the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets execute), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target after generating it. Also, for proper incremental build support, the file should be added to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned up properly.

Let me also invoke the relevant skill since this matches the "including-generated-files" skill perfectly.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates a `.cs` file during build execution, but MSBuild's implicit `<Compile>` glob (`**/*.cs`) expands at **evaluation time** — before any targets run. So the newly created file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Explicitly add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group inside the target (after creating it), and register it in `<FileWrites>` so `dotnet clean` removes it properly.
